questions.scenario: The Orders API cannot reach a private address. Which question belongs before changing firewall rules?

options.text: Does the VPC route table have a path to the destination range or service range?
Can we rename the subnet to private?
Can we grant Viewer to every user?
Can we shorten the service name?

questions.explanation: Routes decide next hop. A firewall allow does not help when no path exists.

questions.scenario: A firewall rule allows TCP 5432, but Cloud Run still times out connecting to a private database. What is the best next check?

options.text: Whether Cloud Run has a VPC egress path and private service route to the database range.
Whether the public domain has a prettier name.
Whether the service account has a longer description.
Whether the database has an emoji label.

questions.explanation: The timeout points to path or service reachability first, especially for Cloud Run private egress.

questions.scenario: Which statements keep routes, firewalls, and IAM separate?

options.text: Routes choose where packets go next.
Firewall rules allow or deny matching network traffic.
IAM controls who may perform API actions.
An IAM role always creates a missing route.

questions.explanation: A clean GCP troubleshooting path keeps route, firewall, and IAM checks distinct.
